name: Linear Algebra
tagline: Vectors, matrices, and transformations that power mechanics, electricity, and machine learning
problem:
As systems grow, scalar arithmetic stops scaling. Forces, currents, measurements, and signals are often multidimensional and coupled. Without a language for vectors and linear transforms, you cannot design or diagnose complex systems reliably.

Linear algebra provides that language and enables computation at scale: solving simultaneous equations, projecting data, and representing transformations compactly.

The remaining problem for Linear Algebra is operational reliability. A reader needs more than a name and a historical note: they need to know what failure this capability prevents, what resources it consumes, what signs show that it is failing, and when it is safer to stop than to improvise. This entry therefore treats linear algebra as a teachable system with inputs, inspection points, records, and limits rather than as a single trick.
overview:
Linear algebra studies vector spaces and linear maps. Matrices represent transformations; eigenvalues describe characteristic modes; decompositions turn messy problems into stable computations.

In engineering and AI, linear algebra is the substrate: sensor fusion, state estimation, circuit analysis, and neural network layers are matrix operations with constraints and numerical stability concerns.

In collapse recovery, linear algebra must be repairable with local materials, teachable to more than one person, and documented well enough that the method survives injury, migration, or leadership change. In a space-settlement context, the same capability must account for closed-loop resources, contamination control, spare-part scarcity, power budgets, and the cost of mistakes in a sealed habitat. The practical minimum is a controlled trial, written checks, safe storage, and review before scale-up.

principles.name: Vector spaces
principles.explanation: A set of objects with addition and scaling; coordinates are representations, not the thing itself.

principles.name: Linear maps
principles.explanation: Transformations that preserve addition and scaling; representable as matrices in a basis.

principles.name: Decompositions
principles.explanation: Factorizations (LU/QR/SVD) solve systems and reveal structure; stability matters more than elegance.

components.name: Use-case boundary
components.function: Defines what Linear Algebra is meant to accomplish and what it must not be used for
components.madeFrom: Written requirements, hazard notes, local constraints, and responsible operator assignment
components.criticalNote: A vague use case leads to unsafe improvisation.

components.name: Input control point
components.function: Checks materials, tools, information, and environmental conditions before work starts
components.position: At receiving, storage, or setup area
components.madeFrom: Labels, inspection notes, gauges, samples, and quarantine bins
components.criticalNote: Unknown inputs should be isolated until checked.

rawMaterials.name: Paper and writing media
rawMaterials.purpose: Record calculations, assumptions, and experimental outcomes
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Digital notes plus printed backups; radiation-safe storage for critical references.

rawMaterials.name: Measurement tools
rawMaterials.purpose: Quantify reality so models are not guesswork
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Prefer multi-use instruments; maintain calibration artifacts and procedures.

rawMaterials.name: Time and attention
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Training time is a hard constraint; plan curricula and drills early.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Deliberate practice, peer review, and documentation.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

buildSteps.title: Learn matrix operations with physical meaning
buildSteps.description: Connect vectors to forces/currents and matrices to coupled equations; avoid purely symbolic memorization.
buildSteps.warningNote: Unit mistakes mimic math mistakes; track units.

buildSteps.title: Practice solving linear systems
buildSteps.description: Use elimination and stable decompositions; verify with residual checks.

buildSteps.title: Use decompositions for stability
buildSteps.description: Prefer QR/SVD for least squares and noisy data; document assumptions.
buildSteps.warningNote: Naive inversion is often a bug.

buildSteps.title: Define the local purpose and stop conditions
buildSteps.description: Write down why Linear Algebra is needed, who is responsible, what success looks like, and which conditions require stopping or escalation.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not start a life-critical or infrastructure-critical use without clear stop conditions.

buildSteps.title: Inspect inputs before use
buildSteps.description: Check materials, tools, water, power, workspace, records, and operator readiness. Mark substitutions and unknowns before work begins.
buildSteps.warningNote: Substitutions can introduce contamination, fire, structural, electrical, medical, or chemical hazards.

history.event: Matrix methods and determinants formalize for physics and engineering.
Numerical linear algebra becomes central to computing.
Linear algebra accelerates data science and ML.

impact:
Linear algebra is the grammar of modern engineering: it turns coupled systems into solvable forms and enables scalable computation.

In The Codex, it is a cross-cutting prerequisite node: mechanics, electricity, navigation, and AI all lean on it.
